headline: Copper at an 18-month high · LME stocks at 4-year low
deck: Chilean supply constraints meet Chinese grid-build demand. Graves has been long since March 4; the curve has been in moderate backwardation through the move.
body: Three-month LME copper closed at 4.81/lb, an 18-month high. Total LME warehouse stocks fell another 11kt this week to 96kt — within shouting distance of the 2021 trough.

Two compounding stories: Chilean supply tightness from union-strike risk at Antofagasta and grid-build pull from China's Q2 infrastructure cycle. Graves has been long physical since March 4 and added to longs at 4.55.
What the desk is watching: the backwardation curve. The front-month / 3M spread has been positive but flat for two weeks; a steepening would signal physical scarcity moving from forecast to fact.

evidence_box.fragment: Copper 3M closes 4.81 — an 18-month high, +4.7% on the week.
On-warrant stocks drawn down a fifth consecutive week.
Graves' long-copper call, opened 4 March; full posterior history attached.
